topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The discovery happened over a misplaced charging cable. I was tidying up Leo’s room, a task I usually approached with a detached efficiency, but today, something snagged my attention. Tucked deep within a drawer, beneath a stack of graphic novels, was a login screen. It was a profile picture of a stylized, anonymous silhouette, and the handle was something cryptic, a string of numbers and symbols that made my stomach clench. My breath hitched. I slowly pulled the device out, my hands trembling slightly as I navigated to the profile. The feed was a carefully curated highlight reel of moments I hadn't been privy to—glamorous outings, inside jokes that excluded me, and an effortless popularity that felt like a physical blow. A tightness constricted my chest, making it hard to draw a full breath. When I finally looked up, Leo was in the hallway, whistling, oblivious. The silence between us felt cavernous, filled with unspoken knowledge.

[story 2]

I found the evidence while scrolling through old cloud backups, a routine digital archaeology project that had spiraled into something far more invasive. It was a secondary account, hidden behind layers of security I hadn't even known existed. The content was raw, unfiltered, and intensely personal—a stream of anxieties, sharp critiques of friends, and an almost desperate need for external validation. Reading it felt like watching someone else's most vulnerable heart laid bare. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the muscle twitching beneath my skin. I slammed the laptop shut, the sound echoing sharply in the quiet house. I stood there, leaning against the doorframe, my knuckles white where I gripped the edge of the desk. Every nerve ending felt hyper-alert, a buzzing static that wouldn't subside, leaving a hollow ache behind my eyes.

[story 3]

It started subtly—a pattern of late-night phone use, a sudden defensiveness when I asked about his screen time. Then, I found the password. It wasn't obvious; it was layered, a deliberate obfuscation. Clicking through the layers felt like trespassing. The photos and posts were a mosaic of curated perfection, juxtaposed jarringly against the mundane reality of our shared life. A wave of profound unease washed over me, cold and heavy. I traced the timeline with my finger, observing the performative nature of it all. A deep, nauseating wave of inadequacy rose within me; all my efforts to connect with him felt suddenly transparent, inadequate against this manufactured persona. I backed away slowly, feeling dizzy, the air suddenly too thin to sustain me.
